skill.name: voice-agents
skill.description: Guidance for designing voice AI agents with speech-to-speech and pipeline architectures, latency budgets, turn taking, voice activity detection, and barge-in handling.
skill.summary: Design low-latency voice agents with practical architecture and conversation patterns.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported four external command alerts at SKILL.md:68 and one weak cryptography alert at SKILL.md:3. Manual review found these are false positives: line 68 is Markdown inline text listing related skills, and line 3 is a plain description of voice agent architecture. No prompt injection, data exfiltration, executable code, or confirmed dangerous behavior was found.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Inline Related Skill Names
low_findings.description: The static analyzer reported Ruby or shell backtick execution four times at SKILL.md:68. The line contains Markdown inline code around related skill names and does not instruct the agent to execute a command.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The referenced line is visible Markdown text for related skills. There is no executable script, shell command, or user input path at that location.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptography Match
low_findings.description: The static analyzer reported a weak cryptographic algorithm at SKILL.md:3. The line is frontmatter description text about voice agent architecture and contains no cryptographic function, hash algorithm, cipher, or security implementation.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The finding maps to descriptive prose, not code. No evidence of MD5, SHA-1, DES, RC4, or similar weak cryptographic usage appears in the referenced file.

content.value_statement: Voice agent projects often fail because latency, turn taking, and audio edge cases are handled too late. This skill helps Claude, Codex, and Claude Code plan practical voice architectures with clear tradeoffs.
actual_capabilities: Compare speech-to-speech and pipeline voice agent architectures.
Plan latency budgets for speech recognition, model response, and speech synthesis.
Identify turn-taking, voice activity detection, and barge-in requirements.
Suggest spoken-response constraints for natural voice conversations.
Highlight common production risks such as noise handling and STT errors.
limitations: Does not provide a complete runnable voice agent implementation.
Does not benchmark vendor latency or audio quality in the current environment.
Does not replace product, privacy, or telephony compliance review.
Mentions OpenAI Realtime API but does not include current API reference details.

use_cases.title: Choose a Voice Architecture
use_cases.description: Compare speech-to-speech and STT-to-LLM-to-TTS designs before implementation begins.

use_cases.title: Plan Conversation Latency
use_cases.description: Create a latency budget that keeps spoken interactions responsive and natural.

use_cases.title: Review Voice UX Risks
use_cases.description: Check designs for interruption handling, noise tolerance, and spoken response length.

prompt_templates.title: Pick an Architecture
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me choose between speech-to-speech and pipeline voice agent architecture for my use case. Ask for missing requirements first.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Latency Budget
prompt_templates.prompt: Build a latency budget for a voice agent that uses STT, an LLM, and TTS. Include target ranges and tradeoffs.

prompt_templates.title: Review Turn Taking
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this voice agent design for turn taking, voice activity detection, interruption handling, and background noise risks.

prompt_templates.title: Harden a Production Flow
prompt_templates.prompt: Audit this production voice agent plan for latency, failure modes, STT recovery, response length, monitoring, and user escalation paths.

output_examples.input: I need a voice assistant for appointment scheduling.
output_examples.output: A concise architecture recommendation with latency targets, turn-taking requirements, interruption handling notes, and risks for noisy calls.

output_examples.input: Review this pipeline voice agent design before launch.
output_examples.output: A structured review covering STT accuracy, LLM response length, TTS timing, barge-in support, monitoring, and fallback behavior.

output_examples.input: Should I use a realtime speech model or separate STT and TTS?
output_examples.output: A tradeoff summary that compares naturalness, control, debugging, latency, cost, and implementation complexity.

best_practices: Set a latency budget before selecting vendors or model architecture.
Design for barge-in, noisy audio, and speech recognition recovery from the start.
Keep spoken responses short, direct, and easy to interrupt.
anti_patterns: Treating a voice agent like a text chatbot with audio added later.
Relying only on silence duration for turn detection.
Writing long responses that sound natural in text but fail in speech.

faq.question: What does this skill help build?
faq.answer: It helps plan voice AI agents that use speech-to-speech or STT-to-LLM-to-TTS architecture.

faq.question: Does it generate production code?
faq.answer: No. It provides architecture guidance, review prompts, and implementation considerations.

faq.question: Which tools can use this skill?
faq.answer: The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.

faq.question: Is it specific to one vendor?
faq.answer: No. It mentions OpenAI Realtime API as one example, but the patterns apply more broadly.

faq.question: What is the main design constraint?
faq.answer: Latency is the main constraint because each speech and model step affects conversation quality.

faq.question: Is this skill safe to publish?
faq.answer: Yes. Manual review found only false positive static findings and no confirmed dangerous behavior.
